action.reasoning: Observation: Walk task trained for 2M steps in 34 min, mean_reward ~12 (normalized). Only walk checkpoint exists. Analysis: The normalized reward of 12 doesn't tell us actual performance. Need to submit eval to see real scores. Also need to train all 10 tasks - with 9.4h remaining, I should use 500k-1M steps per task with fewer envs. The sequential training approach is too slow. Decision: Submit eval with current walk checkpoint (other tasks score 0), then start training all 10 tasks with 1M steps each using 16 envs. Also check if walk checkpoint actually has good performance.
action.command: ls -la ckpt/ && ls -la ckpt/walk/ 2>/dev/null || echo 'No walk ckpt'
